name: pastevector
description: Paste ChemDraw and other clipboard vector content into Markdown by writing SVG or PNG files and inserting Markdown image links.

pasteVector.destinationTemplate.description: Output path template, relative to the current document directory.

pasteVector.preferBackend.description: Linux clipboard backend selection. auto prefers Wayland when available, otherwise X11.

pasteVector.altText.description: Alt text used when inserting the Markdown image.

pasteVector.showLog.description: Show the pasteVector output channel while the command runs.

pasteVector.copyMarkdownToClipboard.description: Also copy the inserted Markdown image link to the VS Code clipboard.

pasteVector.finalizeSvgWithInkscape.description: Run Inkscape on pasted SVG text to fit the canvas and save as plain SVG. Does not affect EMF conversion (use pasteVector.finalizeEmfWithInkscape for that).

pasteVector.emfScalePercent.description: EMF DPI scale factor (e.g. 125 for 125%). After EMF→SVG conversion the root SVG size is multiplied by 100/emfScalePercent. Applies on all platforms. Set to 100 to disable correction.

pasteVector.finalizeEmfWithInkscape.description: After emf2svg-conv conversion, run an Inkscape pass to fit the page canvas exactly to the drawing (including stroke widths) and export as plain SVG. Adds ~1-3s per paste. Has no effect if Inkscape is not in PATH.

pasteVector.replaceDefaultPaste.description: Replace ctrl+V (cmd+V on macOS) in Markdown files with pasteVector. Vector clipboard content (SVG, EMF, PNG) is pasted as an image link; all other content falls back to a normal paste.
